name: paper-mhc-scaling
description: Use web_search to search for 'mHC manifold hyper-connections large scale training foundation models scalability results'. Results overlap with earlier searches. How does mHC perform at large scale? Summarize scalability results and training stability improvements. Keep response under 100 words.

ground_truth.reference_answer: mHC was evaluated at 3B, 9B, and 27B parameter scales on the DeepSeek-V3 MoE architecture with Multi-Head Latent Attention. The relative loss improvement over the baseline remained consistent and even slightly increased at larger compute budgets, demonstrating robust scalability. Token scaling on the 3B model trained on 1 trillion tokens showed stable loss improvement from early training through convergence. At 27B scale, mHC outperformed both baseline and HC: BBH improved from 43.8% (baseline) to 51.0% (mHC), DROP from 47.0% to 53.9%, GSM8K from 46.7% to 53.8%, and MMLU from 59.0% to 63.4%. HC's training divergence at 27B was completely eliminated.
key_facts: Evaluated at 3B, 9B, and 27B parameter scales with consistent relative loss improvements at each scale
27B benchmark results: BBH 51.0% (vs 43.8% baseline), DROP 53.9% (vs 47.0%), GSM8K 53.8% (vs 46.7%), MMLU 63.4% (vs 59.0%)
Token scaling on 3B model trained on 1T tokens showed stable improvement from early training through convergence
Performance advantages were maintained and slightly increased at higher compute budgets
Built on DeepSeek-V3 MoE architecture with Multi-Head Latent Attention and 4 residual streams
